son of Minneapolis, has been visit
ing relatives near Port Townsend,
fear she has been killed by cou-
gar. She visited neighbor last
Sunday and has not been seen
since. In going and coming she had
to pass through woods in which
cougars are known to abound.


Judge c. Victor Martin is on trial
at Wenatchee, charged with having
been guilty of unprofessional con-
duet, of having violated the state
law relative to barratry, violating
his oath as an attorney at law, and
Of having practiced law while act
ing as judge of the superior court.
Judge Poindexter of Spokane Is
hearing the case.


Ed. Mckinley, an aged prospec-
tor and formerly of Butte, Mont,
was found dead in his cabin near
Chattanooga, Colorado, last Friday
night. The man had evidently been
dead for two weeks. Mountain rats
had gnawed off the ends of his
fingers. 1t is believed he starved
to death.


F. Aug. Henize has returned to
Butte from New York where he
consumated the deal whereby all
his properties in litigation were
sold to the Amalgamated Copper
company. Mr.. Heinze says his home
is in Montana and that in a short
time he will be mining as much
copper and employing as large a
number of men as ever.


Ballard is shocked by the hor-
rible act of an unknown man. The
body of a baby was thrown into
the furnace of Stimson's mill at
that city last night. 1t is not known
as yet whether the child was alive
or dead when the act was commit
ted, but this will be determined at
the coroner's inkuest. The night
Waterman saw the act and pulled
the child out. it proved to be a
male about three weeks old.


Within a year Pasco will be one
of the principal division points on
the Northern Pacific railway. 1t is
said that the new division will be
known as the Washington division,
and will include the main line of
the Northern Pacific from Pasco to
Ellensburg and the Sunnyside
branch, the road to Portland along
the north bank of the Columbia
river, also the line up the Snake
river to Riparia. 1t is believed the
Milwaukee will enter Pasco and al.
SO the North Coast railway.
